response:
Use the App Gateway + WAF triage and mitigation pattern we follow for fazure-mcp:

1. Gather AGW logs first
   - Pull AGWAccessLogs and AGWFirewallLogs for the host and timeframe to see HTTP status distribution (500/502/403) and firewall rule hits.

2. Triage backend vs gateway/WAF
   - If there are backend 5xx errors, escalate to backend teams.
   - If primarily 403s or firewall rule hits, continue WAF-focused debugging.

3. Inspect firewall hits and request details
   - Note which custom rules and managed-rule IDs fired (VpnOnly, RateLimit, BlockKnownPath, OWASP rules like 920230/931130/942xxx/949110).
   - Inspect RequestUri (including query string), request body/ARGS (e.g., params.arguments.query), headers (tenant isolation headers), and client IP.

4. Verify allowlists and per-site policy correctness
   - Confirm RackspaceVPN and JanusAWS IPs are present in the WAF allow rules attached to the listener/site.
   - Ensure any per-site WAF policy copied managedRules/policySettings from the global policy and did not omit global Allow rules.

5. Reproduce and validate
   - Perform controlled tests from trusted VPN IPs (health -> 200, /mcp -> 401 baseline) and targeted bursts to check RateLimit and rule behavior.

6. Apply targeted mitigations
   - Adjust custom rule priorities or add exceptions (e.g., move BlockKnownPath priority, add AllowSafeFilters).
   - Add missing trusted IPs to allowlists (full Janus AWS set if needed).
   - Add managed-rule exclusions for problematic ARGS/body fields or remove body inspection where appropriate.

7. Monitor and iterate
   - Verify fixes in AGW logs and App Insights/Log Analytics; confirm no recurrence and that legitimate traffic flows.

This reproducible WAF mitigation/debug pattern (logs → triage → inspect rule hits & request details → validate allowlists/policy cloning → reproduce → apply targeted rule/policy fixes → monitor) is the standard we follow for fazure-mcp.

In AGWAccessLogs for fazure-mcp.mscloud.rackspace.com over the prior 24h, there were 0 HTTP 500 responses, multiple 502s concentrated around 13:46-13:50 UTC and 14:14-14:15 UTC and especially 14:38-14:51 UTC, and a small number of 403s. AGWFirewallLogs showed trusted Rackspace/Janus traffic matching AllowTrustedVpnFazureMcp and current 403s were mainly expected VpnOnly blocks from external/scanner or preview IPs such as 146.70.189.237 and 52.123.190.89/125. T", "2026-04-14: On 2026-04-14, after new MCP 403s from Janus AWS IPs, added the full Janus AWS address set 52.39.2.74, 52.41.136.221, 52.10.115.132, and 52.40.141.99 to the trusted IP allowlists for fazure MCP and oauth endpoints on Azure Application Gateway. Root cause was that recent POST /mcp failures on fazure-mcp.mscloud.rackspace.com at 2026-04-14T17:51:41Z through 2026-04-14T18:06:13Z were blocked by custom rule VpnOnly under policy scope fazure-mcp, even though those IPs are the known JanusAWS set already present in the global policy. Fixed live by updating site policy waf-appgw-phoenix-prod-southcent", "2026-03-25: On 2026-03-25, investigated fazure-mcp WAF 403s for POST /mcp from Rackspace VPN IP 72.32.180.181. Local Azure-MCP rollout artifacts showed the per-listener WAF policy waf-appgw-phoenix-prod-southcentralus-rax-fazure-mcp-sites was created by copying managedRules and policySettings from the global policy, then attaching only site custom rules RateLimit, VpnOnly, BlockSensitiveFiles, BlockKnownPath, GeoBlocking, and AllowSafeFilters. The global RackspaceVPN Allow rule was not copied into the site policy. Result: trusted VPN traffic still traverses OWASP CRS 3.2 managed rules on /mcp and legitima", "2026-03-25: On 2026-03-25, a reusable WAF mitigation pattern was established for trusted internal MCP SQL traffic behind Azure Application Gateway. Rackspace VPN IP 72.32.180.181 was receiving 403s on POST /mcp to fazure-mcp.mscloud.rackspace.com because the per-listener WAF policy waf-appgw-phoenix-prod-southcentralus-rax-fazure-mcp-sites allowed the VPN through the VpnOnly rule but still sent the JSON request body through OWASP managed inspection. AGWAccessLogs in workspace ecae2296-e4c2-47f2-9336-24599caea6be showed repeated GET /authorize requests returning 403 between 2026-03-25T14:40:58Z and 2026-03-25T14:41:34Z. Baseline responses from the VPN client were /health -> 200 and /mcp -> 401. A 130-request burst to /mcp completed with 130 HTTP 401 responses and the immediate follow-up request also returned 401. Log Analytics confirmed 132 /mcp requests with HttpStatus 401 in the 2026-03-19T19:39:00Z minute for ClientIp 72.32.180.181.
